title: Issue with `podman play kube` on Ubuntu
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

On Ubuntu, the E2e test `podman generate with user and reimport kube on pod` ([link](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/e2e/generate_kube_test.go#L256)) fails. We have temporarily disabled the test.

The error message is:
```
Error: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "error adding seccomp rule for syscall socket: requested action matches default action of filter": OCI runtime error
```

I believe this is related to the recent vendor of https://github.com/seccomp/containers-golang/pull/27 into Podman. The error message in question is generated by runc when trying to add a seccomp rule whose action matches the default action of the policy; my current theory is that the specific errno number in that PR is being dropped, causing the rule to just have ActErrno - which is exactly identical to the default action.

It is presently unclear why this only occurs on one test for `podman play kube`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create an Ubuntu 20.04 VM

2. Clone Podman repository and install all dependencies

3. Run E2E tests

**Describe the results you received:**

Test fails

**Describe the results you expected:**

Test succeeds

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Core difference here appears to be crun (Fedora) vs runc (Ubuntu)

**Output of `podman version`:**

Latest master

comments.body: I think we need an updated runc that has: https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/2424

`/usr/bin/runc` on Ubuntu is owned by the distro default runc. I have set dependencies to use distro runc by default since that seemed good enough for most cases. Would it be ok to let this be until Ubuntu updates its runc?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe @lsm5 Is this still an issue, has the runc been  updated?  Should we switch to crun?
Still being skipped on Ubuntu. I'll make a PR to try reenabling and see if it still explodes.
